questions.question: What is a major advantage of a journaling file system like ext4?
answers: It encrypts all user credentials automatically
It speeds up graphics card rendering rates
It compresses text files to half their initial disk sector allocations
It records changes in a log to prevent corruption during sudden power losses

questions.question: What is the primary role of the Virtual File System (VFS) layer in a kernel?
answers: To buffer incoming network package flows before delivery to sockets
To manage virtual memory swap spaces on physical storage disks
To provide an abstract uniform interface for applications to access diverse file systems
To simulate internal hard drive arrays inside local ram spaces

questions.question: What structural pattern does a Hard Link follow compared to a Symbolic Link?
answers: Symbolic links can only reference binary files; hard links handle folders
Hard links require administrative privileges to execute
A Hard Link points directly to the same inode; a Symbolic Link points to a path string
Hard links delete the original file target when broken; symbolic links do not

questions.question: What does an inode store in a Unix-like file system?
answers: The literal plain-text contents of the target file
File metadata, size, permissions, and block pointers, but not the file name
The encryption keys used for secure network transmissions
The human-readable file name and directory path string

questions.question: What is the main difference between internal and external fragmentation?
answers: Internal is fixed by defragmentation; external requires virtual memory flushes
Internal occurs inside fixed allocated blocks; external occurs between variable blocks
External happens inside kernel space; internal happens in user space
External happens on hard drives; internal happens inside CPU registers

questions.question: What is the role of a Translation Lookaside Buffer (TLB)?
answers: To act as a specialized cache for virtual-to-physical address translations
To map file allocation records sequentially on disk
To handle network data package buffering streams
To store execution metrics logs for background tasks

questions.question: Which scheduling algorithm runs processes based on a fixed time-slice quantum rotation?
answers: Shortest Job First (SJF)
First-Come, First-Served (FCFS)
Round Robin (RR)
Multilevel Feedback Queue

questions.question: What state does a process enter if it is waiting for an I/O operation to finish?
answers: Zombie state
Running state
Ready state
Blocked / Waiting state

questions.question: What differentiates a process from a thread?
answers: A process has its own isolated address space; threads share address spaces
A single thread can contain multiple independent processes
Threads run in kernel mode; processes run in user mode
Processes share file descriptors by default; threads do not

questions.question: What is a major disadvantage of the Shortest Job First scheduling algorithm?
answers: High context-switching processing overhead
Massive memory cache utilization footprints
It requires specialized graphical hardware layers
Long-running processes can suffer from starvation
